secret-scanning.title: List secret scanning alerts for an enterprise

parameters.name: enterprise
parameters.description: The slug version of the enterprise name.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Set to open or resolved to only list secret scanning alerts in a specific state.

parameters.name: secret_type
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of secret types to return. All default secret patterns are returned. To return generic patterns, pass the token name(s) in the parameter. See "Supported secret scanning patterns" for a complete list of secret types.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of resolutions. Only secret scanning alerts with one of these resolutions are listed. Valid resolutions are false_positive, wont_fix, revoked, pattern_edited, pattern_deleted or used_in_tests.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The property to sort the results by. created means when the alert was created. updated means when the alert was updated or resolved.

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: The direction to sort the results by.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: validity
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of validities that, when present, will return alerts that match the validities in this list. Valid options are active, inactive, and unknown.

parameters.name: is_publicly_leaked
parameters.description: A boolean value representing whether or not to filter alerts by the publicly-leaked tag being present.

parameters.name: is_multi_repo
parameters.description: A boolean value representing whether or not to filter alerts by the multi-repo tag being present.

parameters.name: hide_secret
parameters.description: A boolean value representing whether or not to hide literal secrets in the results.

secret-scanning.descriptionHTML: Lists secret scanning alerts for eligible repositories in an enterprise, from newest to oldest. To use this endpoint, you must be a member of the enterprise, and you must use an access token with the repo scope or security_events scope. Alerts are only returned for organizations in the enterprise for which you are an organization owner or a security manager, or for personal account repositories.

created_at.description: The time that the alert was created in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

anyOf.description: The time that the alert was last updated in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

url.description: The REST API URL of the alert resource.

html_url.description: The GitHub URL of the alert resource.

locations_url.description: The REST API URL of the code locations for this alert.

state.description: Sets the state of the secret scanning alert. You must provide `resolution` when you set the state to `resolved`.

resolution.description: **Required when the `state` is `resolved`.** The reason for resolving the alert.

resolved_at.description: The time that the alert was resolved in ISO 8601 format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`.

secret_type.description: The type of secret that secret scanning detected.

secret_type_display_name.description:
User-friendly name for the detected secret, matching the `secret_type`.
For a list of built-in patterns, see "[Supported secret scanning patterns](https://docs.github.com/enterprise-server@3.19/code-security/secret-scanning/introduction/supported-secret-scanning-patterns#supported-secrets)."

id.description: A unique identifier of the repository.

node_id.description: The GraphQL identifier of the repository.

html_url.description: The URL to view the repository on GitHub.com.

deployments_url.description: The API URL to list the deployments of the repository.

downloads_url.description: The API URL to list the downloads on the repository.

events_url.description: The API URL to list the events of the repository.

forks_url.description: The API URL to list the forks of the repository.

git_refs_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git refs of the repository.

git_tags_url.description: A template for the API URL to get information about Git tags of the repository.

secret-scanning.descriptionHTML:
Lists secret scanning alerts for eligible repositories in an organization, from newest to oldest.

The authenticated user must be an administrator or security manager for the organization to use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or security_events scope to use this endpoint. If this endpoint is only used with public repositories, the token can use the public_repo scope instead.

Lists requests to dismiss secret scanning alerts in an org.

Delegated alert dismissal must be enabled on repositories in the org and the user must be an org admin, security manager, or have the "Review and manage secret scanning alert dismissal requests" permission to access this endpoint.
